metadata.title: SEC EDGAR primary-source figures for the AI-capex cluster
metadata.provenance: Primary EDGAR XBRL API + filing-text pulls (data.sec.gov/api/xbrl/companyconcept + companyfacts; curl with SEC User-Agent). Figures marked confidence=high are primary-filing-derived; med/low are press/knowledge fills clearly noted.

filings.notes: NVIDIA tags revenue as us-gaap:Revenues and capex as PaymentsToAcquireProductiveAssets post-FY2020.
NVIDIA is capital-LIGHT (capex ~3% of revenue) - it sells the picks-and-shovels; the capex burden sits on its customers (hyperscalers/neoclouds), which is the structural core of the circularity.
Two direct customers each >25% (likely the OEM/distributor channel masking hyperscaler + neocloud demand). High concentration risk.
Commercial RPO; surged on Azure/OpenAI-related multi-year commitments.
Additions to property & equipment (pure capex, excl. finance leases). FY2024 = $44,477M. Q1 FY26 P&E capex $19.4B; ~$34.9B incl. finance leases.
$3.1B drag / $0.41 EPS hit from OpenAI equity-method share. Prior-year Q1 FY25 was only $523M / $0.07 - the loss is scaling fast as OpenAI burns.
RPO went $130.2B (yr ago) -> $455B -> $552.6B. ONLY ~12% recognizable within 12 months: heavily back-loaded, dominated by the ~$300B OpenAI/Stargate contract. The backlog is a promise, not cash.

Notes payable non-current $124.7B + current $9.887B. Up from $92.6B at FY25 end. ~$44B+ new bond issuance to fund OpenAI-related datacenter buildout - debt-financing a single back-loaded customer contract.
Backlog $55.6B (Q3 2025) -> $66.8B (Dec 2025). An XBRL RPO tag also surfaced ~$98.8B (may include later OpenAI expansion/contracted-not-RPO). Customer concentration extreme (Microsoft ~62% FY24).
UNRESOLVED: Amazon switched the XBRL tag; old tag ends 2020. AWS RPO reported in the low-mid $200B range in press; not primary-verified here.
MSFT+GOOGL+AMZN+META+ORCL combined ~$380-420B CY2025; 2026 guidance points toward ~$500-650B. Knowledge/guidance, not a single filing line.

Useful life EXTENDED to 5.5 years effective Jan 1 2025 (from ~4-5). Extending depreciable life lowers annual depreciation -> inflates reported operating income now while pushing the GPU-obsolescence reckoning out.

Computer/server equipment 2-6 years. All four hyperscalers extended useful lives 2023-2025 - a synchronized, earnings-flattering accounting choice against hardware (Blackwell-era GPUs) that may obsolesce in 3-4 years.

spacex_s1_status.UPDATED: 2026-06-07: the S-1 went public. As of 2026-06-06 no S-1 was on EDGAR (it was still confidential DRS). See fin-spacex-spcx.json for full detail.
spacex_s1_status.detail: SpaceX S-1 filed 2026-05-20 (CIK 0001181412), S-1/A No.1 on 2026-06-01; ticker SPCX on Nasdaq/Nasdaq Texas. Reported terms: ~$1.75T-$2T valuation, ~$75B all-primary raise, ~$135/share, 555.6M shares. 2025 revenue $18.67B (Starlink/connectivity ~$10-11.4B, launch ~$6.4B, Starshield ~$1.8B); 10.3M Starlink subs; ~50% YoY growth. KEY: days before pricing, SpaceX signed CANCELABLE AI-compute reselling deals (Google $920M/mo; Anthropic $1.25B/mo; both terminable on 90-day notice after Dec 31 2026) - see fin-spacex-spcx.json.
